title: The Shoe Shiner's Rhythm
text_zh: 火车站旁边有一个擦鞋的老人。他擦鞋的时候有一种特别的节奏——左三下，右三下，然后用布擦一遍。每一双鞋他都这样擦，从来不变。我坐在那里等他擦鞋的时候，发现他的动作比很多音乐家都准确。他说他干这行已经三十年了。「三十年就做一件事，你想做不好都难。」我觉得他说得对。现在的人总是想做很多事情，但是很少有人能把一件事做到这么好。他的节奏里有一种别人学不来的安定——那是时间送给他的礼物。
text_pinyin: Huǒchē zhàn pángbiān yǒu yí gè cā xié de lǎorén. Tā cā xié de shíhou yǒu yì zhǒng tèbié de jiézòu——zuǒ sān xià, yòu sān xià, ránhòu yòng bù cā yí biàn. Měi yì shuāng xié tā dōu zhèyàng cā, cónglái bú biàn. Wǒ zuò zài nàlǐ děng tā cā xié de shíhou, fāxiàn tā de dòngzuò bǐ hěn duō yīnyuèjiā dōu zhǔnquè. Tā shuō tā gàn zhè háng yǐjīng sānshí nián le.「Sānshí nián jiù zuò yí jiàn shì, nǐ xiǎng zuò bù hǎo dōu nán.」 Wǒ juéde tā shuō de duì. Xiànzài de rén zǒngshì xiǎng zuò hěn duō shìqing, dànshì hěn shǎo yǒu rén néng bǎ yí jiàn shì zuò dào zhème hǎo. Tā de jiézòu lǐ yǒu yì zhǒng biéren xué bù lái de āndìng——nà shì shíjiān sòng gěi tā de lǐwù.
text_en: Next to the train station, there's an old man who shines shoes. He has a special rhythm when polishing — three strokes left, three strokes right, then one wipe with a cloth. He does every pair this way, never changing. Sitting there waiting, I noticed his movements were more precise than many musicians'. He said he's done this for thirty years. 'Thirty years doing one thing — it's hard not to get good at it.' I think he's right. People today always want to do many things, but few can do one thing this well. His rhythm holds a steadiness others can't learn — it's a gift that time gave him.

title: The Rain-Watching Couple
text_zh: 下大雨的时候，我在一家小面馆里躲雨。旁边坐着一对年轻人，他们吃完面以后没有走，就坐在窗边一起看雨。女生把头靠在男生的肩膀上，两个人都不说话。外面的雨越下越大，但是他们好像一点都不着急。面馆老板走过来说：「不赶时间就坐着吧，反正今天也没什么客人。」他给他们倒了两杯热茶。我看着窗外的雨，突然觉得下雨天也不是什么坏事。因为雨给了我们一个理由，可以什么都不做，只是安安静静地坐一会儿。
text_pinyin: Xià dà yǔ de shíhou, wǒ zài yì jiā xiǎo miànguǎn lǐ duǒ yǔ. Pángbiān zuòzhe yí duì niánqīng rén, tāmen chī wán miàn yǐhòu méiyǒu zǒu, jiù zuò zài chuāng biān yìqǐ kàn yǔ. Nǚshēng bǎ tóu kào zài nánshēng de jiānbǎng shàng, liǎng gè rén dōu bù shuō huà. Wàimiàn de yǔ yuè xià yuè dà, dànshì tāmen hǎoxiàng yìdiǎn dōu bù zhāojí. Miànguǎn lǎobǎn zǒu guòlái shuō:「Bù gǎn shíjiān jiù zuòzhe ba, fǎnzhèng jīntiān yě méi shénme kèrén.」 Tā gěi tāmen dào le liǎng bēi rè chá. Wǒ kànzhe chuāng wài de yǔ, tūrán juéde xià yǔ tiān yě búshì shénme huài shì. Yīnwèi yǔ gěi le wǒmen yí gè lǐyóu, kěyǐ shénme dōu bú zuò, zhǐshì ān'ānjìngjìng de zuò yíhuìr.
text_en: During a heavy rain, I took shelter in a small noodle shop. A young couple sat nearby. After finishing their noodles, they didn't leave — just sat by the window watching the rain together. The girl rested her head on the boy's shoulder. Neither spoke. The rain outside grew heavier, but they didn't seem to be in any hurry. The shop owner came over and said: 'If you're not in a rush, just sit — not many customers today anyway.' He poured them two cups of hot tea. Looking at the rain outside, I suddenly felt rainy days aren't so bad. Because rain gives us a reason to do nothing at all, and just sit quietly for a while.

title: The Morning Market Grandmother
text_zh: 早市上有一个卖菜的奶奶，她的菜摊比别人的小，但是每样菜都摆得整整齐齐的。黄瓜一根一根地排好，西红柿按大小分成三堆。我问她为什么要花这么多时间摆菜。她说：「菜也有自己的样子。摆得好看，买的人也会更珍惜。」旁边的菜贩子说她太慢了，这样赚不了钱。但是她不在乎。每天早上，她的摊子前面总是排着几个老顾客。他们不只是来买菜的——他们是来看她把普通的菜变成一幅画的。有时候认真对待一件小事，本身就是一种美。
text_pinyin: Zǎoshì shàng yǒu yí gè mài cài de nǎinai, tā de càitān bǐ biéren de xiǎo, dànshì měi yàng cài dōu bǎi de zhěngzhěngqíqí de. Huángguā yì gēn yì gēn de pái hǎo, xīhóngshì àn dàxiǎo fēn chéng sān duī. Wǒ wèn tā wèishénme yào huā zhème duō shíjiān bǎi cài. Tā shuō:「Cài yě yǒu zìjǐ de yàngzi. Bǎi de hǎokàn, mǎi de rén yě huì gèng zhēnxī.」 Pángbiān de càifànzi shuō tā tài màn le, zhèyàng zhuàn bù liǎo qián. Dànshì tā bú zàihū. Měi tiān zǎoshang, tā de tānzi qiánmiàn zǒngshì páizhe jǐ gè lǎo gùkè. Tāmen bù zhǐshì lái mǎi cài de——tāmen shì lái kàn tā bǎ pǔtōng de cài biàn chéng yì fú huà de. Yǒu shíhou rènzhēn duìdài yí jiàn xiǎo shì, běnshēn jiùshì yì zhǒng měi.
text_en: At the morning market, there's a grandmother who sells vegetables. Her stall is smaller than others', but every vegetable is arranged neatly. Cucumbers lined up one by one, tomatoes sorted by size into three piles. I asked why she spends so much time arranging them. She said: 'Vegetables have their own appearance too. When they look nice, the buyer will treasure them more.' The vendor next to her says she's too slow and can't make money this way. But she doesn't care. Every morning, a few regular customers line up at her stall. They're not just buying vegetables — they come to watch her turn ordinary produce into a painting. Sometimes taking a small thing seriously is itself a kind of beauty.

title: The Elevator Conversation
text_zh: 我住在十八楼。每天坐电梯的时候，都会遇到不同的人。大部分时候大家都看手机，不说话。但是有一个住在十二楼的老爷爷，每次见到人都会说一句话。比如下雨天他会说「记得带伞」，冬天他会说「今天穿得够不够」。一开始我觉得他有点奇怪。但是后来我发现，因为他每天说的这些话，整个楼的人都慢慢地开始互相打招呼了。电梯里的气氛比以前温暖了很多。一个人的善意，就像一颗被扔进水里的石头——波纹会慢慢地扩散到你看不到的地方。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zhù zài shíbā lóu. Měi tiān zuò diàntī de shíhou, dōu huì yù dào bùtóng de rén. Dà bùfen shíhou dàjiā dōu kàn shǒujī, bù shuō huà. Dànshì yǒu yí gè zhù zài shí'èr lóu de lǎo yéye, měi cì jiàn dào rén dōu huì shuō yí jù huà. Bǐrú xià yǔ tiān tā huì shuō「jìde dài sǎn」, dōngtiān tā huì shuō「jīntiān chuān de gòu bú gòu」. Yì kāishǐ wǒ juéde tā yǒudiǎn qíguài. Dànshì hòulái wǒ fāxiàn, yīnwèi tā měi tiān shuō de zhèxiē huà, zhěng gè lóu de rén dōu mànman de kāishǐ hùxiāng dǎ zhāohu le. Diàntī lǐ de qìfēn bǐ yǐqián wēnnuǎn le hěn duō. Yí gè rén de shànyì, jiù xiàng yì kē bèi rēng jìn shuǐ lǐ de shítou——bōwén huì mànman de kuòsàn dào nǐ kàn bú dào de dìfang.
text_en: I live on the eighteenth floor. Every day in the elevator, I meet different people. Most of the time everyone looks at their phones without speaking. But there's a grandpa on the twelfth floor who always says something to people. On rainy days he says 'remember your umbrella.' In winter he says 'did you dress warm enough?' At first I thought he was a bit odd. But later I noticed that because of his daily words, everyone in the building gradually started greeting each other. The atmosphere in the elevator became much warmer. One person's kindness is like a stone thrown into water — the ripples slowly spread to places you can't see.

title: The Retired Dancer
text_zh: 每天早上，公园的角落里有一个老太太在跳舞。她不是跟大妈们一起跳广场舞，而是一个人在那里跳芭蕾。她的动作很慢，但是每一个转身都很优雅。有人说她以前是舞蹈演员。我有一天走过去跟她聊天。她说她跳了四十年的舞，退休以后身体不如从前了，但是她每天还是要跳一个小时。「不是为了表演，」她说，「是因为我的身体记得那些动作。如果我不跳了，那些记忆就会慢慢消失。」我看着她在晨光里慢慢地旋转，觉得那是我见过最安静、最美的舞蹈。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang, gōngyuán de jiǎoluò lǐ yǒu yí gè lǎo tàitai zài tiào wǔ. Tā búshì gēn dàmāmen yìqǐ tiào guǎngchǎng wǔ, érshì yí gè rén zài nàlǐ tiào bālěi. Tā de dòngzuò hěn màn, dànshì měi yí gè zhuǎnshēn dōu hěn yōuyǎ. Yǒu rén shuō tā yǐqián shì wǔdǎo yǎnyuán. Wǒ yǒu yì tiān zǒu guòqù gēn tā liáotiān. Tā shuō tā tiào le sìshí nián de wǔ, tuìxiū yǐhòu shēntǐ bùrú cóngqián le, dànshì tā měi tiān háishi yào tiào yí gè xiǎoshí.「Búshì wèile biǎoyǎn,」tā shuō,「shì yīnwèi wǒ de shēntǐ jìde nàxiē dòngzuò. Rúguǒ wǒ bù tiào le, nàxiē jìyì jiù huì mànman xiāoshī.」 Wǒ kànzhe tā zài chénguāng lǐ mànman de xuánzhuǎn, juéde nà shì wǒ jiàn guò zuì ānjìng, zuì měi de wǔdǎo.
text_en: Every morning in a park corner, an old woman dances. She doesn't join the square-dancing aunties — she dances ballet alone. Her movements are slow, but every turn is elegant. People say she was once a dancer. One day I went to chat with her. She said she danced for forty years. Since retiring, her body isn't what it was, but she still dances an hour every day. 'Not for performance,' she said, 'but because my body remembers those movements. If I stop dancing, those memories will slowly disappear.' Watching her slowly spin in the morning light, I thought it was the quietest, most beautiful dance I've ever seen.

title: The Last Bookmark
text_zh: 爷爷去世以后，我在整理他的书架。每一本书里都夹着一个书签——有的是旧报纸剪的，有的是干了的树叶，有的是用过的车票。最后一本书是一本关于园艺的书，书签夹在第一百二十三页。那一页上有一句话被他用铅笔画了线：「花不会因为没有人看它就不开。」我把那个书签拿出来——是一张我小时候画的画，画的是爷爷在花园里浇花。我不记得什么时候画的了，但是他一直留着。有些人走了以后，他们留下的东西会继续说着他们没说完的话。
text_pinyin: Yéye qùshì yǐhòu, wǒ zài zhěnglǐ tā de shūjià. Měi yì běn shū lǐ dōu jiāzhe yí gè shūqiān——yǒu de shì jiù bàozhǐ jiǎn de, yǒu de shì gān le de shùyè, yǒu de shì yòng guò de chē piào. Zuìhòu yì běn shū shì yì běn guānyú yuányì de shū, shūqiān jiā zài dì yìbǎi èrshísān yè. Nà yí yè shàng yǒu yí jù huà bèi tā yòng qiānbǐ huà le xiàn:「Huā bú huì yīnwèi méiyǒu rén kàn tā jiù bù kāi.」 Wǒ bǎ nàge shūqiān ná chūlái——shì yì zhāng wǒ xiǎo shíhou huà de huà, huà de shì yéye zài huāyuán lǐ jiāo huā. Wǒ bú jìde shénme shíhou huà de le, dànshì tā yìzhí liúzhe. Yǒuxiē rén zǒu le yǐhòu, tāmen liú xià de dōngxi huì jìxù shuōzhe tāmen méi shuō wán de huà.
text_en: After grandpa passed away, I was organizing his bookshelf. Every book had a bookmark — some cut from old newspapers, some dried leaves, some used bus tickets. The last book was about gardening, with a bookmark at page one hundred twenty-three. On that page, he'd underlined a sentence in pencil: 'A flower doesn't stop blooming just because no one is watching it.' I took out the bookmark — it was a drawing I made as a child, of grandpa watering flowers in the garden. I don't remember when I drew it, but he'd kept it all along. After some people are gone, the things they leave behind continue saying the words they never finished.

title: The Bridge Over the Canal
text_zh: 我每天上班都要经过一座老桥。这座桥已经有五十多年的历史了。桥面上的石头被走过的人磨得很光滑。早上七点，桥上全是赶着去上班的人，走得很快。但是到了下午，桥上的人就变了——有的人停下来看河里的水，有的人靠在桥栏上发呆。我喜欢下班以后慢慢地走过这座桥。桥下面的水很绿，偶尔有一条小船经过。有时候我会想，这座桥看过了多少人来人去，听过了多少故事。它什么都不说，只是安安静静地在那里，让每一个走过的人都能稍微停一停。
text_pinyin: Wǒ měi tiān shàng bān dōu yào jīngguò yí zuò lǎo qiáo. Zhè zuò qiáo yǐjīng yǒu wǔshí duō nián de lìshǐ le. Qiáo miàn shàng de shítou bèi zǒu guò de rén mó de hěn guānghuá. Zǎoshang qī diǎn, qiáo shàng quán shì gǎnzhe qù shàng bān de rén, zǒu de hěn kuài. Dànshì dào le xiàwǔ, qiáo shàng de rén jiù biàn le——yǒu de rén tíng xiàlái kàn hé lǐ de shuǐ, yǒu de rén kào zài qiáo lán shàng fādāi. Wǒ xǐhuan xiàbān yǐhòu mànman de zǒu guò zhè zuò qiáo. Qiáo xiàmiàn de shuǐ hěn lǜ, ǒu'ěr yǒu yì tiáo xiǎo chuán jīngguò. Yǒu shíhou wǒ huì xiǎng, zhè zuò qiáo kàn guò le duōshao rén lái rén qù, tīng guò le duōshao gùshi. Tā shénme dōu bù shuō, zhǐshì ān'ānjìngjìng de zài nàlǐ, ràng měi yí gè zǒu guò de rén dōu néng shāowēi tíng yì tíng.
text_en: Every day going to work I cross an old bridge. This bridge is over fifty years old. The stones on its surface have been worn smooth by all the people who've walked over them. At seven in the morning, the bridge is full of people rushing to work, walking fast. But in the afternoon, the people change — some stop to look at the water, some lean on the railing and daydream. I like slowly crossing the bridge after work. The water below is green, and occasionally a small boat passes. Sometimes I wonder how many people coming and going this bridge has watched, how many stories it's heard. It says nothing, just quietly sits there, letting everyone who crosses pause for a moment.

title: The Laundry on the Balcony
text_zh: 从我家窗户看出去，能看到对面楼的很多阳台。每个阳台上都晒着衣服。有一家晒的全是小孩子的衣服——很小，很可爱，颜色鲜艳。旁边一家晒的是深色的工作服，整整齐齐的。再过去一家，只有一件白衬衫在风里慢慢地飘。我有时候觉得，不用进别人的家就能知道他们的生活。那些衣服像是无声的自我介绍——一个有小孩的家庭，一个认真工作的人，一个独自生活的人。城市把我们关在各自的房间里，但是阳台上的衣服让我们看到彼此。
text_pinyin: Cóng wǒ jiā chuānghu kàn chūqù, néng kàn dào duìmiàn lóu de hěn duō yángtái. Měi gè yángtái shàng dōu shàizhe yīfu. Yǒu yì jiā shài de quán shì xiǎo háizi de yīfu——hěn xiǎo, hěn kě'ài, yánsè xiānyàn. Pángbiān yì jiā shài de shì shēnsè de gōngzuòfú, zhěngzhěngqíqí de. Zài guòqù yì jiā, zhǐ yǒu yí jiàn bái chènshān zài fēng lǐ mànman de piāo. Wǒ yǒu shíhou juéde, bú yòng jìn biéren de jiā jiù néng zhīdào tāmen de shēnghuó. Nàxiē yīfu xiàng shì wúshēng de zìwǒ jièshào——yí gè yǒu xiǎohái de jiātíng, yí gè rènzhēn gōngzuò de rén, yí gè dúzì shēnghuó de rén. Chéngshì bǎ wǒmen guān zài gèzì de fángjiān lǐ, dànshì yángtái shàng de yīfu ràng wǒmen kàn dào bǐcǐ.
text_en: Looking out my window, I can see many balconies on the building across. Every balcony has laundry drying. One family's clothes are all children's — small, cute, brightly colored. The neighbor's are dark work uniforms, neatly arranged. Next door, just one white shirt floating slowly in the wind. Sometimes I think you can know people's lives without entering their homes. Those clothes are like silent introductions — a family with children, a hardworking person, someone living alone. The city locks us each in our own rooms, but the laundry on balconies lets us see one another.

title: The Neon Sign That Stayed
text_zh: 我们街上有一家老理发店，门口有一个很旧的霓虹灯招牌。招牌上面的字有几个已经不亮了，只能看到「理发」两个字。旁边新开的店都用LED灯，又亮又好看。有人建议老板换一个新招牌，他摇摇头说：「这个招牌是我爸爸做的，比我还老。它在这条街上亮了四十年了，我不想让它消失。」每天晚上，那个旧招牌发出暗暗的红光。跟旁边的新灯比起来，它确实不够亮。但是在我看来，那种旧旧的红光比任何新灯都有味道。它不只是一块招牌——它是这条街的记忆。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen jiē shàng yǒu yì jiā lǎo lǐfà diàn, ménkǒu yǒu yí gè hěn jiù de níhóngdēng zhāopai. Zhāopai shàngmiàn de zì yǒu jǐ gè yǐjīng bú liàng le, zhǐ néng kàn dào「lǐfà」liǎng gè zì. Pángbiān xīn kāi de diàn dōu yòng LED dēng, yòu liàng yòu hǎokàn. Yǒu rén jiànyì lǎobǎn huàn yí gè xīn zhāopai, tā yáo yao tóu shuō:「Zhège zhāopai shì wǒ bàba zuò de, bǐ wǒ hái lǎo. Tā zài zhè tiáo jiē shàng liàng le sìshí nián le, wǒ bù xiǎng ràng tā xiāoshī.」 Měi tiān wǎnshang, nàge jiù zhāopai fāchū àn'àn de hóngguāng. Gēn pángbiān de xīn dēng bǐ qǐlái, tā quèshí bú gòu liàng. Dànshì zài wǒ kàn lái, nà zhǒng jiùjiù de hóngguāng bǐ rènhé xīn dēng dōu yǒu wèidào. Tā bù zhǐshì yí kuài zhāopai——tā shì zhè tiáo jiē de jìyì.
text_en: On our street there's an old barbershop with a very old neon sign out front. Some of the characters no longer light up — you can only see 'haircut.' New shops nearby all use LED lights, bright and pretty. Someone suggested the owner get a new sign. He shook his head: 'My father made this sign. It's older than me. It's been glowing on this street for forty years — I don't want it to disappear.' Every evening, the old sign gives off a dim red glow. Compared to the new lights next door, it's definitely not bright enough. But to me, that old red glow has more character than any new light. It's not just a sign — it's this street's memory.

title: The Vending Machine at Midnight
text_zh: 我家楼下有一台自动贩卖机，二十四小时都亮着。半夜的时候，它是整条街上唯一的光。有时候我睡不着，就下楼去买一瓶水。不是因为渴，只是想听那个硬币掉下去的声音，然后听到水瓶掉出来的「咚」。在安静的夜里，那个声音让人觉得安心。有一次我看到一个穿着拖鞋的女生也来买东西。我们互相看了一眼，都没说话，但是那一刻我觉得不那么孤单了。半夜买东西的人，大概都有自己睡不着的原因。那台贩卖机不会问你为什么来——它只是给你一瓶水，和一点安静的陪伴。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā lóu xià yǒu yì tái zìdòng fànmàijī, èrshísì xiǎoshí dōu liàngzhe. Bànyè de shíhou, tā shì zhěng tiáo jiē shàng wéiyī de guāng. Yǒu shíhou wǒ shuì bù zháo, jiù xià lóu qù mǎi yì píng shuǐ. Búshì yīnwèi kě, zhǐshì xiǎng tīng nàge yìngbì diào xiàqù de shēngyīn, ránhòu tīng dào shuǐ píng diào chūlái de「dōng」. Zài ānjìng de yè lǐ, nàge shēngyīn ràng rén juéde ānxīn. Yǒu yí cì wǒ kàn dào yí gè chuānzhe tuōxié de nǚshēng yě lái mǎi dōngxi. Wǒmen hùxiāng kàn le yì yǎn, dōu méi shuō huà, dànshì nà yí kè wǒ juéde bú nàme gūdān le. Bànyè mǎi dōngxi de rén, dàgài dōu yǒu zìjǐ shuì bù zháo de yuányīn. Nà tái fànmàijī bú huì wèn nǐ wèishénme lái——tā zhǐshì gěi nǐ yì píng shuǐ, hé yìdiǎn ānjìng de péibàn.
text_en: Downstairs from my home there's a vending machine, lit twenty-four hours a day. At midnight, it's the only light on the street. Sometimes when I can't sleep, I go downstairs to buy a bottle of water. Not because I'm thirsty — just to hear the coin drop, then the 'thud' of the bottle coming out. In the quiet night, that sound is reassuring. Once I saw a girl in slippers also buying something. We glanced at each other, neither spoke, but in that moment I felt less alone. People buying things at midnight probably all have their own reasons for sleeplessness. The vending machine won't ask why you came — it just gives you a bottle of water and a bit of quiet company.

title: The Construction Site Lullaby
text_zh: 我家旁边有一个建筑工地。白天很吵，到处是机器的声音。但是到了晚上，工地安静下来以后，偶尔能听到有人在唱歌。是一个男人的声音，唱的好像是一首摇篮曲。我后来才知道，那是一个建筑工人，他的孩子在老家，他每天晚上都对着手机给孩子唱歌哄睡觉。他的声音不好听，但是很温柔。隔着一堵墙，我听着那首歌，也觉得安心了。有时候，城市里最动人的声音不在音乐厅里，而是在一个你看不到的角落里，一个想念孩子的父亲轻轻唱着的歌。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā pángbiān yǒu yí gè jiànzhù gōngdì. Báitiān hěn chǎo, dàochù shì jīqì de shēngyīn. Dànshì dào le wǎnshang, gōngdì ānjìng xiàlái yǐhòu, ǒu'ěr néng tīng dào yǒu rén zài chàng gē. Shì yí gè nánrén de shēngyīn, chàng de hǎoxiàng shì yì shǒu yáolánqǔ. Wǒ hòulái cái zhīdào, nà shì yí gè jiànzhù gōngrén, tā de háizi zài lǎojiā, tā měi tiān wǎnshang dōu duìzhe shǒujī gěi háizi chàng gē hǒng shuì jiào. Tā de shēngyīn bù hǎotīng, dànshì hěn wēnróu. Gézhe yì dǔ qiáng, wǒ tīngzhe nà shǒu gē, yě juéde ānxīn le. Yǒu shíhou, chéngshì lǐ zuì dòngrén de shēngyīn bú zài yīnyuè tīng lǐ, érshì zài yí gè nǐ kàn bú dào de jiǎoluò lǐ, yí gè xiǎngniàn háizi de fùqīn qīngqīng chàngzhe de gē.
text_en: Next to my home there's a construction site. Daytime is noisy, machines everywhere. But at night, after the site quiets down, sometimes you can hear someone singing. A man's voice, what sounds like a lullaby. I later learned it's a construction worker whose child is back in his hometown. Every night he sings to his child through his phone to lull them to sleep. His voice isn't pretty, but it's very gentle. Through the wall, listening to that song, I too feel at ease. Sometimes the most moving sounds in a city aren't in concert halls, but in a corner you can't see, where a father missing his child sings softly.

title: The Museum Guard's Secret
text_zh: 博物馆里有一个保安，每天都站在同一幅画前面。我以为他是被安排在那里的。但是有一天我跟他聊天，他说那幅画是他最喜欢的——是一幅很安静的风景画，画的是一个小村庄和远处的山。他说：「我小时候住在一个很像这幅画的地方。来城市工作以后，再也没有回去过。但是每天看到这幅画，就好像回到了小时候。」他在这个博物馆工作了十年，每天看同一幅画，从来不觉得厌。他说这不只是一幅画，是他回家的路。
text_pinyin: Bówùguǎn lǐ yǒu yí gè bǎo'ān, měi tiān dōu zhàn zài tóng yí fú huà qiánmiàn. Wǒ yǐwéi tā shì bèi ānpái zài nàlǐ de. Dànshì yǒu yì tiān wǒ gēn tā liáotiān, tā shuō nà fú huà shì tā zuì xǐhuan de——shì yì fú hěn ānjìng de fēngjǐng huà, huà de shì yí gè xiǎo cūnzhuāng hé yuǎnchù de shān. Tā shuō:「Wǒ xiǎo shíhou zhù zài yí gè hěn xiàng zhè fú huà de dìfang. Lái chéngshì gōngzuò yǐhòu, zài yě méiyǒu huíqù guò. Dànshì měi tiān kàn dào zhè fú huà, jiù hǎoxiàng huí dào le xiǎo shíhou.」 Tā zài zhège bówùguǎn gōngzuò le shí nián, měi tiān kàn tóng yí fú huà, cónglái bù juéde yàn. Tā shuō zhè bù zhǐshì yì fú huà, shì tā huí jiā de lù.
text_en: In the museum there's a guard who stands before the same painting every day. I assumed he was assigned there. But one day I chatted with him. He said that painting is his favorite — a quiet landscape of a small village and distant mountains. He said: 'As a child I lived in a place just like this painting. After coming to the city for work, I never went back. But seeing this painting every day feels like returning to childhood.' He's worked at this museum for ten years, looking at the same painting daily, never tiring of it. He says it's not just a painting — it's his road home.

title: The School Nurse's Drawer
text_zh: 我们学校的校医室不大，只有一张床和一张桌子。校医是一个很温柔的中年女人。除了给学生看病以外，她的抽屉里还有很多「特别的药」：巧克力、棒棒糖、创可贴上面画着卡通图案。她说：「有些学生来校医室不是因为身体不舒服，而是因为心情不好。考试没考好的、跟朋友吵架的、想家的——他们需要的不是药，是有人愿意听他们说几分钟的话。」她的抽屉被学生们叫做「百宝箱」。毕业的时候，很多学生特意去跟她告别。一个校医室，变成了学校里最温暖的角落。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen xuéxiào de xiàoyīshì bú dà, zhǐ yǒu yì zhāng chuáng hé yì zhāng zhuōzi. Xiàoyī shì yí gè hěn wēnróu de zhōngnián nǚrén. Chúle gěi xuéshēng kànbìng yǐwài, tā de chōuti lǐ hái yǒu hěn duō「tèbié de yào」: qiǎokèlì, bàngbàngtáng, chuāngkětiē shàngmiàn huàzhe kǎtōng tú'àn. Tā shuō:「Yǒuxiē xuéshēng lái xiàoyīshì búshì yīnwèi shēntǐ bù shūfu, érshì yīnwèi xīnqíng bù hǎo. Kǎoshì méi kǎo hǎo de, gēn péngyou chǎojià de, xiǎng jiā de——tāmen xūyào de búshì yào, shì yǒu rén yuànyì tīng tāmen shuō jǐ fēnzhōng de huà.」 Tā de chōuti bèi xuéshēngmen jiào zuò「bǎibǎoxiāng」. Bìyè de shíhou, hěn duō xuéshēng tèyì qù gēn tā gàobié. Yí gè xiàoyīshì, biàn chéng le xuéxiào lǐ zuì wēnnuǎn de jiǎoluò.
text_en: Our school's nurse's office is small — just one bed and one desk. The nurse is a gentle middle-aged woman. Besides treating students, her drawer holds many 'special medicines': chocolates, lollipops, band-aids with cartoon patterns. She says: 'Some students come to the nurse's office not because they feel physically ill, but because they're in a bad mood. Failed an exam, fought with a friend, homesick — what they need isn't medicine, it's someone willing to listen for a few minutes.' Students call her drawer the 'treasure chest.' At graduation, many students specifically go to say goodbye to her. A nurse's office became the warmest corner in the school.

title: The Bus Driver's Farewell
text_zh: 每天早上七点十分，我坐的那班公交车总是同一个司机——一个五十多岁的叔叔。他从来不催人上车，到站的时候会等跑过来的人。有一天早上，他在广播里说：「各位乘客，明天开始我就退休了。谢谢你们这么多年坐我的车。」车上突然安静了。然后一个老太太说了一声「谢谢你」。接着，越来越多的人开始说谢谢。那个司机的眼睛有点红了。他说：「开了二十年的车，你们有些人我虽然不认识，但是我认识你们上车的表情。」有些联系不需要名字，也不需要说话。每天在同一个时间做同一件事，本身就是一种承诺。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang qī diǎn shí fēn, wǒ zuò de nà bān gōngjiāo chē zǒngshì tóng yí gè sījī——yí gè wǔshí duō suì de shūshu. Tā cónglái bù cuī rén shàng chē, dào zhàn de shíhou huì děng pǎo guòlái de rén. Yǒu yì tiān zǎoshang, tā zài guǎngbō lǐ shuō:「Gèwèi chéngkè, míngtiān kāishǐ wǒ jiù tuìxiū le. Xièxie nǐmen zhème duō nián zuò wǒ de chē.」 Chē shàng tūrán ānjìng le. Ránhòu yí gè lǎo tàitai shuō le yì shēng「xièxie nǐ」. Jiēzhe, yuè lái yuè duō de rén kāishǐ shuō xièxie. Nàge sījī de yǎnjing yǒudiǎn hóng le. Tā shuō:「Kāi le èrshí nián de chē, nǐmen yǒuxiē rén wǒ suīrán bú rènshi, dànshì wǒ rènshi nǐmen shàng chē de biǎoqíng.」 Yǒuxiē liánxì bù xūyào míngzi, yě bù xūyào shuō huà. Měi tiān zài tóng yí gè shíjiān zuò tóng yí jiàn shì, běnshēn jiùshì yì zhǒng chéngnuò.
text_en: Every morning at seven ten, the bus I take always has the same driver — a man in his fifties. He never rushes passengers, and waits at stops for people running to catch the bus. One morning he announced on the intercom: 'Dear passengers, starting tomorrow I'm retiring. Thank you for riding my bus all these years.' The bus went silent. Then an old lady said 'thank you.' Then more and more people started saying thanks. The driver's eyes turned red. He said: 'After driving for twenty years, some of you I don't know by name, but I know the expressions on your faces when you get on.' Some connections don't need names or words. Doing the same thing at the same time every day is itself a kind of promise.

title: The Temple's Free Hot Water
text_zh: 城郊有一座小寺庙，门口放着一个大热水壶，旁边贴着一张纸条：「免费热水，请自取。」不管冬天还是夏天，那壶热水从早到晚都在。来取水的人各种各样——附近的环卫工人、经过的路人、偶尔还有迷路的游客。寺庙的师父说：「我们做不了大事，但是可以做一壶热水。冬天喝了暖身体，夏天泡一杯茶解解渴。」有人问他这样不是很费钱吗，他笑着说：「一壶水的钱不多，但是喝了的人可能会记得这个地方很久。」简单的善意，有时候比复杂的帮助更持久。
text_pinyin: Chéngjiāo yǒu yí zuò xiǎo sìmiào, ménkǒu fàngzhe yí gè dà rè shuǐ hú, pángbiān tiēzhe yì zhāng zhǐtiáo:「Miǎnfèi rè shuǐ, qǐng zì qǔ.」 Bùguǎn dōngtiān háishi xiàtiān, nà hú rè shuǐ cóng zǎo dào wǎn dōu zài. Lái qǔ shuǐ de rén gè zhǒng gè yàng——fùjìn de huánwèi gōngrén, jīngguò de lùrén, ǒu'ěr hái yǒu mílù de yóukè. Sìmiào de shīfu shuō:「Wǒmen zuò bù liǎo dà shì, dànshì kěyǐ zuò yì hú rè shuǐ. Dōngtiān hē le nuǎn shēntǐ, xiàtiān pào yì bēi chá jiě jie kě.」 Yǒu rén wèn tā zhèyàng búshì hěn fèi qián ma, tā xiàozhe shuō:「Yì hú shuǐ de qián bù duō, dànshì hē le de rén kěnéng huì jìde zhège dìfang hěn jiǔ.」 Jiǎndān de shànyì, yǒu shíhou bǐ fùzá de bāngzhù gèng chíjiǔ.
text_en: On the city outskirts there's a small temple with a large hot water thermos at the entrance and a note: 'Free hot water, please help yourself.' Winter or summer, that hot water is there from morning to night. All sorts of people come — nearby sanitation workers, passersby, occasionally lost tourists. The temple monk says: 'We can't do big things, but we can make a pot of hot water. In winter it warms the body; in summer, brew a cup of tea to quench thirst.' Someone asked if it's too expensive. He smiled: 'A pot of water doesn't cost much, but the person who drinks it might remember this place for a long time.' Simple kindness is sometimes more lasting than complicated help.

title: The Disappearing Flowers
text_zh: 公司门口有一个花坛，每周一都会多出几朵新种的花。但是到了周五，总有一两朵花不见了。大家都很奇怪。有人说是被风吹走了，有人说是有人偷的。我决定早点来看看。周一早上六点，我看到了——一个穿清洁服的阿姨正在种花。她从自己家里带来的。而周五的早上，我又看到她小心地把开得最好的花剪下来，放进一个小花瓶里。我跟着她走，看到她把花送到了旁边养老院一个老人的窗台上。她说：「那个老奶奶以前特别喜欢种花，现在走不动了。我让花去看看她。」
text_pinyin: Gōngsī ménkǒu yǒu yí gè huātán, měi zhōuyī dōu huì duō chū jǐ duǒ xīn zhòng de huā. Dànshì dào le zhōuwǔ, zǒng yǒu yì liǎng duǒ huā bú jiàn le. Dàjiā dōu hěn qíguài. Yǒu rén shuō shì bèi fēng chuī zǒu le, yǒu rén shuō shì yǒu rén tōu de. Wǒ juédìng zǎo diǎn lái kàn kan. Zhōuyī zǎoshang liù diǎn, wǒ kàn dào le——yí gè chuān qīngjié fú de āyí zhèng zài zhòng huā. Tā cóng zìjǐ jiā lǐ dài lái de. Ér zhōuwǔ de zǎoshang, wǒ yòu kàn dào tā xiǎoxīn de bǎ kāi de zuì hǎo de huā jiǎn xiàlái, fàng jìn yí gè xiǎo huāpíng lǐ. Wǒ gēnzhe tā zǒu, kàn dào tā bǎ huā sòng dào le pángbiān yǎnglǎoyuàn yí gè lǎorén de chuāngtái shàng. Tā shuō:「Nàge lǎo nǎinai yǐqián tèbié xǐhuan zhòng huā, xiànzài zǒu bú dòng le. Wǒ ràng huā qù kàn kan tā.」
text_en: Outside the office there's a flower bed. Every Monday new flowers appear. But by Friday, one or two always go missing. Everyone found it strange. Some said wind blew them away; others said someone stole them. I decided to come early. Monday at six AM, I saw — a cleaning lady was planting flowers. She brought them from home. And Friday morning, I watched her carefully cut the best-blooming flowers and place them in a small vase. I followed her and saw her put the flowers on the windowsill of an elderly person at the nursing home next door. She said: 'That grandmother used to love growing flowers. Now she can't walk anymore. So I let the flowers go visit her.'

title: The Extra Lunch Box
text_zh: 每天中午，公司的冰箱里都会多出来一个没有名字的饭盒。饭盒里是一份很简单但是很好吃的便当——有米饭、一个菜、一块肉。没有人知道是谁放的。有人试过吃，说很好吃，像妈妈做的菜。我观察了两个星期，终于发现了——是前台的姐姐。她每天早上早起半个小时给一个新来的实习生做的。那个实习生刚来城市，租的房子没有厨房，天天吃泡面。但是前台姐姐从来没有说是她做的。她说：「如果他知道是我做的，他会不好意思。不知道是谁做的，他吃起来会比较自在。」
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zhōngwǔ, gōngsī de bīngxiāng lǐ dōu huì duō chūlái yí gè méiyǒu míngzi de fànhé. Fànhé lǐ shì yí fèn hěn jiǎndān dànshì hěn hǎochī de biàndāng——yǒu mǐfàn, yí gè cài, yí kuài ròu. Méiyǒu rén zhīdào shì shéi fàng de. Yǒu rén shì guò chī, shuō hěn hǎochī, xiàng māma zuò de cài. Wǒ guānchá le liǎng gè xīngqī, zhōngyú fāxiàn le——shì qiántái de jiějie. Tā měi tiān zǎoshang zǎo qǐ bàn gè xiǎoshí gěi yí gè xīn lái de shíxíshēng zuò de. Nàge shíxíshēng gāng lái chéngshì, zū de fángzi méiyǒu chúfáng, tiāntiān chī pàomiàn. Dànshì qiántái jiějie cónglái méiyǒu shuō shì tā zuò de. Tā shuō:「Rúguǒ tā zhīdào shì wǒ zuò de, tā huì bù hǎoyìsi. Bù zhīdào shì shéi zuò de, tā chī qǐlái huì bǐjiào zìzai.」
text_en: Every noon, an unlabeled lunch box appears in the company fridge. Inside is a simple but delicious bento — rice, one dish, one piece of meat. Nobody knew who put it there. Someone tried it and said it tasted like a mother's cooking. I observed for two weeks and finally discovered — it was the receptionist. She wakes up half an hour early every morning to cook for a new intern. The intern just came to the city, rents a place without a kitchen, and eats instant noodles every day. But the receptionist never says she's the one cooking. She says: 'If he knew it was me, he'd feel embarrassed. Not knowing who made it, he'll eat more comfortably.'

title: The Bookmark Messages
text_zh: 图书馆的一本旧小说里，我发现了一个书签，上面写着：「这本书的结局会让你哭。做好准备。」我笑了笑，继续看。看完以后，我真的哭了。我在书签的另一面写了：「你说得对。我在图书馆哭了，很丢人。」然后把书签放回去。一个月以后我又来借这本书，发现书签还在，但是下面多了一行字：「别不好意思，我当时也哭了。在这里哭的一共有七个人了，你是第八个。」我又在下面写了一行：「算上你写的这行，现在是第九个了吧？」这种不认识的人之间的对话，温柔得像一首诗。
text_pinyin: Túshūguǎn de yì běn jiù xiǎoshuō lǐ, wǒ fāxiàn le yí gè shūqiān, shàngmiàn xiězhe:「Zhè běn shū de jiéjú huì ràng nǐ kū. Zuò hǎo zhǔnbèi.」 Wǒ xiào le xiào, jìxù kàn. Kàn wán yǐhòu, wǒ zhēn de kū le. Wǒ zài shūqiān de lìng yí miàn xiě le:「Nǐ shuō de duì. Wǒ zài túshūguǎn kū le, hěn diūrén.」 Ránhòu bǎ shūqiān fàng huíqù. Yí gè yuè yǐhòu wǒ yòu lái jiè zhè běn shū, fāxiàn shūqiān hái zài, dànshì xiàmiàn duō le yì háng zì:「Bié bù hǎoyìsi, wǒ dāngshí yě kū le. Zài zhèlǐ kū de yígòng yǒu qī gè rén le, nǐ shì dì bā gè.」 Wǒ yòu zài xiàmiàn xiě le yì háng:「Suàn shàng nǐ xiě de zhè háng, xiànzài shì dì jiǔ gè le ba?」 Zhè zhǒng bú rènshi de rén zhījiān de duìhuà, wēnróu de xiàng yì shǒu shī.
text_en: In an old novel at the library, I found a bookmark that read: 'This book's ending will make you cry. Be prepared.' I smiled and kept reading. When I finished, I really did cry. On the other side of the bookmark I wrote: 'You were right. I cried in the library. Embarrassing.' Then put it back. A month later I borrowed the book again. The bookmark was still there, with a new line: 'Don't be embarrassed, I cried too. Seven people have cried here now. You're the eighth.' I added another line: 'Counting your line, isn't it the ninth now?' This kind of conversation between strangers is gentle as a poem.

title: Grandpa's Noodle Soup
text_zh: 爷爷做的面汤很简单——就是清水煮面条，放一点盐，加几滴香油。小时候我不喜欢，觉得没什么味道。长大以后，我在外面吃了很多好吃的面，但是每次生病或者心情不好的时候，我最想吃的还是爷爷的面汤。后来爷爷不在了，我试着自己做，但是怎么做味道都不对。妈妈说：「因为爷爷煮面的时候，会在旁边看着你吃。他的面汤好不好吃，不在于调料，在于那个看着你的人。」现在每次我煮面，都会多放一张椅子在旁边。虽然没有人坐，但是我觉得那碗面的味道比以前好了一点。
text_pinyin: Yéye zuò de miàn tāng hěn jiǎndān——jiùshì qīngshuǐ zhǔ miàntiáo, fàng yìdiǎn yán, jiā jǐ dī xiāngyóu. Xiǎo shíhou wǒ bù xǐhuan, juéde méi shénme wèidào. Zhǎng dà yǐhòu, wǒ zài wàimiàn chī le hěn duō hǎochī de miàn, dànshì měi cì shēngbìng huòzhě xīnqíng bù hǎo de shíhou, wǒ zuì xiǎng chī de háishi yéye de miàn tāng. Hòulái yéye bú zài le, wǒ shìzhe zìjǐ zuò, dànshì zěnme zuò wèidào dōu bú duì. Māma shuō:「Yīnwèi yéye zhǔ miàn de shíhou, huì zài pángbiān kànzhe nǐ chī. Tā de miàn tāng hǎo bù hǎochī, bú zàiyú tiáoliào, zàiyú nàge kànzhe nǐ de rén.」 Xiànzài měi cì wǒ zhǔ miàn, dōu huì duō fàng yì zhāng yǐzi zài pángbiān. Suīrán méiyǒu rén zuò, dànshì wǒ juéde nà wǎn miàn de wèidào bǐ yǐqián hǎo le yìdiǎn.
text_en: Grandpa's noodle soup was simple — plain water, noodles, a bit of salt, a few drops of sesame oil. As a child I didn't like it, thought it had no flavor. Growing up, I ate many delicious noodles outside, but every time I was sick or feeling low, I craved grandpa's noodle soup most. After grandpa was gone, I tried making it myself, but the taste was never right. Mom said: 'Because when grandpa cooked noodles, he'd sit beside you watching you eat. What made his soup taste good wasn't the seasoning — it was the person watching you.' Now every time I cook noodles, I place an extra chair nearby. Nobody sits there, but I feel the noodles taste a little better than before.

title: The Lunch Hour Ritual
text_zh: 我们办公室有一个不成文的规矩：中午十二点，不管多忙，大家都会放下工作，一起去食堂吃饭。老板也不例外。吃饭的时候不谈工作，聊的都是生活上的事——谁的孩子考试了，谁发现了一家好吃的店。有新人问我：「为什么你们公司这么在意吃午饭？」我说：「因为我们老板说过一句话——一个连午饭都不好好吃的公司，做不好其他事。」其实午饭本身不重要，重要的是每天有那么半个小时，大家可以把工作忘掉，像朋友一样说说话。这半个小时让下午的工作比较容易坚持。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen bàngōngshì yǒu yí gè bù chéngwén de guījǔ: zhōngwǔ shí'èr diǎn, bùguǎn duō máng, dàjiā dōu huì fàng xià gōngzuò, yìqǐ qù shítáng chī fàn. Lǎobǎn yě bú lìwài. Chī fàn de shíhou bù tán gōngzuò, liáo de dōu shì shēnghuó shàng de shì——shéi de háizi kǎoshì le, shéi fāxiàn le yì jiā hǎochī de diàn. Yǒu xīnrén wèn wǒ:「Wèishénme nǐmen gōngsī zhème zàiyì chī wǔfàn?」 Wǒ shuō:「Yīnwèi wǒmen lǎobǎn shuō guò yí jù huà——yí gè lián wǔfàn dōu bù hǎohāo chī de gōngsī, zuò bù hǎo qítā shì.」 Qíshí wǔfàn běnshēn bú zhòngyào, zhòngyào de shì měi tiān yǒu nàme bàn gè xiǎoshí, dàjiā kěyǐ bǎ gōngzuò wàng diào, xiàng péngyou yíyàng shuō shuō huà. Zhè bàn gè xiǎoshí ràng xiàwǔ de gōngzuò bǐjiào róngyì jiānchí.
text_en: Our office has an unwritten rule: at noon, no matter how busy, everyone stops work and goes to the cafeteria together. The boss too. During meals, no work talk — just life: whose kid had a test, who found a good restaurant. A newcomer asked me: 'Why does your company care so much about lunch?' I said: 'Because our boss once said — a company that can't even eat lunch properly can't do anything else well.' Actually lunch itself isn't important. What matters is that half hour every day when everyone can forget work and talk like friends. That half hour makes the afternoon work easier to get through.

title: The Neighborhood Dumpling Day
text_zh: 我们小区每年冬至都有一个传统：大家在一楼大厅一起包饺子。桌子是物业搬出来的，面和馅儿是每家每户凑的。有的人包得很好看，有的人包得像云朵一样奇怪，但是没有人笑话谁。包完以后大家一起煮、一起吃。每次都有人说：「自己家包的没有这么好吃。」大概是因为一百个人的手做出来的饺子，每一个都不一样，但是每一个里面都有一点邻居的温度。有些味道不是食材给的，而是一群人坐在一起的那种感觉给的。冬至的饺子不只是食物——它是一个整个小区围在一起的理由。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen xiǎoqū měi nián Dōngzhì dōu yǒu yí gè chuántǒng: dàjiā zài yī lóu dàtīng yìqǐ bāo jiǎozi. Zhuōzi shì wùyè bān chūlái de, miàn hé xiànr shì měi jiā měi hù còu de. Yǒu de rén bāo de hěn hǎokàn, yǒu de rén bāo de xiàng yúnduǒ yíyàng qíguài, dànshì méiyǒu rén xiàohua shéi. Bāo wán yǐhòu dàjiā yìqǐ zhǔ, yìqǐ chī. Měi cì dōu yǒu rén shuō:「Zìjǐ jiā bāo de méiyǒu zhème hǎochī.」 Dàgài shì yīnwèi yì bǎi gè rén de shǒu zuò chūlái de jiǎozi, měi yí gè dōu bù yíyàng, dànshì měi yí gè lǐmiàn dōu yǒu yìdiǎn línjū de wēndù. Yǒuxiē wèidào búshì shícái gěi de, érshì yì qún rén zuò zài yìqǐ de nà zhǒng gǎnjué gěi de. Dōngzhì de jiǎozi bù zhǐshì shíwù——tā shì yí gè zhěng gè xiǎoqū wéi zài yìqǐ de lǐyóu.
text_en: Every winter solstice, our complex has a tradition: everyone gathers in the first-floor lobby to make dumplings together. Tables come from property management; flour and filling are contributed by each household. Some people wrap beautifully, some make cloud-shaped oddities, but nobody laughs at anyone. After wrapping, everyone cooks and eats together. Someone always says: 'The ones at home never taste this good.' Probably because dumplings made by a hundred pairs of hands are each different, but each one carries a bit of a neighbor's warmth. Some flavors don't come from ingredients — they come from the feeling of people sitting together. Winter solstice dumplings aren't just food — they're a reason for the whole community to gather.

title: The Tea Shop's Unspoken Rule
text_zh: 老城区有一家小茶馆，只有六张桌子。老板有一个规矩：如果你一个人来，他会问你要不要跟别人拼桌。不是因为桌子不够，而是因为他觉得「喝茶是要有人陪的」。一开始我觉得有点奇怪，但是拼桌以后，我跟一个退休老师聊了一个下午。他给我讲了很多这条街以前的故事。老板说：「现在大家都对着手机说话，很少跟面前的人说话了。我这个茶馆虽然小，但是我想让来的人放下手机，跟旁边的人聊聊。」也许这就是好茶馆和普通茶馆的区别——好的茶馆不只是卖茶，而是创造了一个让人说话的地方。
text_pinyin: Lǎo chéngqū yǒu yì jiā xiǎo cháguǎn, zhǐ yǒu liù zhāng zhuōzi. Lǎobǎn yǒu yí gè guījǔ: rúguǒ nǐ yí gè rén lái, tā huì wèn nǐ yào bu yào gēn biéren pīn zhuō. Búshì yīnwèi zhuōzi bú gòu, érshì yīnwèi tā juéde「hē chá shì yào yǒu rén péi de」. Yì kāishǐ wǒ juéde yǒudiǎn qíguài, dànshì pīn zhuō yǐhòu, wǒ gēn yí gè tuìxiū lǎoshī liáo le yí gè xiàwǔ. Tā gěi wǒ jiǎng le hěn duō zhè tiáo jiē yǐqián de gùshi. Lǎobǎn shuō:「Xiànzài dàjiā dōu duìzhe shǒujī shuō huà, hěn shǎo gēn miànqián de rén shuō huà le. Wǒ zhège cháguǎn suīrán xiǎo, dànshì wǒ xiǎng ràng lái de rén fàng xià shǒujī, gēn pángbiān de rén liáo liao.」 Yěxǔ zhè jiùshì hǎo cháguǎn hé pǔtōng cháguǎn de qūbié——hǎo de cháguǎn bù zhǐshì mài chá, érshì chuàngzào le yí gè ràng rén shuō huà de dìfang.
text_en: In the old district there's a small tea house with just six tables. The owner has a rule: if you come alone, he asks if you'd like to share a table. Not because there aren't enough tables, but because he believes 'tea should be drunk with company.' At first I found it odd, but after sharing a table, I chatted with a retired teacher all afternoon. He told me many stories about the street's past. The owner says: 'Now everyone talks to their phone, rarely to the person in front of them. My tea house is small, but I want people who come to put down their phones and chat with the person beside them.' Perhaps that's the difference between a good tea house and an ordinary one — a good tea house doesn't just sell tea, it creates a place where people talk.

title: The Guesthouse Guestbook
text_zh: 我住的民宿有一本留言本。到的第一个晚上，我翻了翻。有人画了一幅小画，是窗外的山。有人写了一首诗。有一个人只写了四个字：「我很累。来到这里以后，终于可以休息了。」看到这句话的时候，我突然觉得眼睛有点酸。因为我也是同样的原因来的。工作太忙了，很久没有好好休息了。这家民宿没有电视，没有网络，只有窗外的山和虫子的声音。第二天早上我在留言本上写了一句：「感谢这里的安静。它比任何药都有用。」有时候我们需要的不是去一个远方，而是找到一个可以安安静静待着的地方。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zhù de mínsù yǒu yì běn liúyán běn. Dào de dì yī gè wǎnshang, wǒ fān le fān. Yǒu rén huà le yì fú xiǎo huà, shì chuāng wài de shān. Yǒu rén xiě le yì shǒu shī. Yǒu yí gè rén zhǐ xiě le sì gè zì:「Wǒ hěn lèi. Lái dào zhèlǐ yǐhòu, zhōngyú kěyǐ xiūxi le.」 Kàn dào zhè jù huà de shíhou, wǒ tūrán juéde yǎnjing yǒudiǎn suān. Yīnwèi wǒ yě shì tóngyàng de yuányīn lái de. Gōngzuò tài máng le, hěn jiǔ méiyǒu hǎohāo xiūxi le. Zhè jiā mínsù méiyǒu diànshì, méiyǒu wǎngluò, zhǐ yǒu chuāng wài de shān hé chóngzi de shēngyīn. Dì èr tiān zǎoshang wǒ zài liúyán běn shàng xiě le yí jù:「Gǎnxiè zhèlǐ de ānjìng. Tā bǐ rènhé yào dōu yǒu yòng.」 Yǒu shíhou wǒmen xūyào de búshì qù yí gè yuǎnfāng, érshì zhǎo dào yí gè kěyǐ ān'ānjìngjìng dāizhe de dìfang.
text_en: The guesthouse I stayed at had a guestbook. The first night, I flipped through it. Someone drew a small picture of the mountains outside the window. Someone wrote a poem. One person wrote just four characters: 'I'm very tired. After coming here, I can finally rest.' Reading that, my eyes suddenly stung. Because I came for the same reason. Work was too busy; I hadn't properly rested in ages. This guesthouse has no TV, no internet — just mountains outside and the sound of insects. The next morning I wrote in the guestbook: 'Thank you for the quiet here. It's more useful than any medicine.' Sometimes what we need isn't going somewhere far away, but finding a place where we can simply be still.

title: The Train Station Piano
text_zh: 在一个欧洲的火车站里，候车大厅放着一架公共钢琴。任何人都可以坐下来弹。我在等车的时候，看到一个背着大书包的年轻人坐了下来。他弹了一首很简单的曲子，不太好听，但是弹完以后他笑了。然后一个穿西装的中年人坐了下来，弹了一首古典音乐，非常好听。大家都停下来听。弹完以后他站起来，拿起行李箱就走了，好像什么都没发生过。我在那里坐了一个小时，看了五个不同的人弹琴。每个人弹的都不一样，但是每个人弹完以后的表情都是一样的——放松、满足。也许旅行中最好的时刻，不是到达目的地，而是等待的时候突然听到的一首曲子。
text_pinyin: Zài yí gè Ōuzhōu de huǒchē zhàn lǐ, hòuchē dàtīng fàngzhe yí jià gōnggòng gāngqín. Rènhé rén dōu kěyǐ zuò xiàlái tán. Wǒ zài děng chē de shíhou, kàn dào yí gè bēizhe dà shūbāo de niánqīng rén zuò le xiàlái. Tā tán le yì shǒu hěn jiǎndān de qǔzi, bú tài hǎotīng, dànshì tán wán yǐhòu tā xiào le. Ránhòu yí gè chuān xīzhuāng de zhōngnián rén zuò le xiàlái, tán le yì shǒu gǔdiǎn yīnyuè, fēicháng hǎotīng. Dàjiā dōu tíng xiàlái tīng. Tán wán yǐhòu tā zhàn qǐlái, ná qǐ xíngli xiāng jiù zǒu le, hǎoxiàng shénme dōu méi fāshēng guò. Wǒ zài nàlǐ zuò le yí gè xiǎoshí, kàn le wǔ gè bùtóng de rén tánqín. Měi gè rén tán de dōu bù yíyàng, dànshì měi gè rén tán wán yǐhòu de biǎoqíng dōu shì yíyàng de——fàngsōng, mǎnzú. Yěxǔ lǚxíng zhōng zuì hǎo de shíkè, búshì dàodá mùdìdì, érshì děngdài de shíhou tūrán tīng dào de yì shǒu qǔzi.
text_en: In a European train station, there's a public piano in the waiting hall. Anyone can sit and play. While waiting for my train, I watched a young person with a big backpack sit down. He played a simple piece, not very good, but smiled after finishing. Then a suited middle-aged man sat down and played a classical piece, beautifully. Everyone stopped to listen. When he finished, he stood, grabbed his suitcase, and walked away as if nothing happened. I sat there for an hour, watching five different people play. Each played differently, but everyone's expression after finishing was the same — relaxed, content. Perhaps the best moment in travel isn't arriving at the destination, but a piece of music you suddenly hear while waiting.

title: The Map That Got Wet
text_zh: 在一个陌生的城市旅行，我的手机没电了，只有一张纸质地图。然后下雨了。地图被雨淋湿了，看不清楚了。我站在路边不知道怎么走。一个卖伞的老人看到我，走过来说：「你要去哪里？」我告诉他以后，他笑着说：「地图看不清没关系，跟着我走就行。」他带我穿过了好几条小巷子，走了大概十分钟。到了以后他说：「年轻人，有时候迷路也不是坏事。如果你没有迷路，你就不会走进这些巷子，也不会认识我。」他说完就走了，消失在雨里。那是我那次旅行中最温暖的十分钟。
text_pinyin: Zài yí gè mòshēng de chéngshì lǚxíng, wǒ de shǒujī méi diàn le, zhǐ yǒu yì zhāng zhǐzhì dìtú. Ránhòu xià yǔ le. Dìtú bèi yǔ lín shī le, kàn bù qīngchu le. Wǒ zhàn zài lù biān bù zhīdào zěnme zǒu. Yí gè mài sǎn de lǎorén kàn dào wǒ, zǒu guòlái shuō:「Nǐ yào qù nǎlǐ?」 Wǒ gàosu tā yǐhòu, tā xiàozhe shuō:「Dìtú kàn bù qīng méi guānxi, gēnzhe wǒ zǒu jiù xíng.」 Tā dài wǒ chuān guò le hǎo jǐ tiáo xiǎo xiàngzi, zǒu le dàgài shí fēnzhōng. Dào le yǐhòu tā shuō:「Niánqīng rén, yǒu shíhou mílù yě búshì huài shì. Rúguǒ nǐ méiyǒu mílù, nǐ jiù bú huì zǒu jìn zhèxiē xiàngzi, yě bú huì rènshi wǒ.」 Tā shuō wán jiù zǒu le, xiāoshī zài yǔ lǐ. Nà shì wǒ nà cì lǚxíng zhōng zuì wēnnuǎn de shí fēnzhōng.
text_en: Traveling in an unfamiliar city, my phone died. I only had a paper map. Then it rained. The map got soaked and became unreadable. I stood by the road not knowing which way to go. An old umbrella seller saw me and came over: 'Where are you going?' After I told him, he smiled: 'Can't read the map? No problem, just follow me.' He led me through several alleys, about ten minutes' walk. When we arrived he said: 'Young person, sometimes getting lost isn't a bad thing. If you hadn't gotten lost, you wouldn't have walked through these alleys, and you wouldn't have met me.' Then he left, disappearing into the rain. Those were the warmest ten minutes of that trip.
